title: Xi'an 10-Day Heritage Tour
metaTitle: Xi'an 10-Day Heritage Tour | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 10-day trip to Xi'an, China: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Terracotta Army and Xi'an City Wall bike ride. Prices from $1761 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 10 days discovering Xi'an, China — Semi-humid continental climate — hot summers (highs around 32-33°C) and cold, dry winters (highs around 6-7°C). March-May and September-November, for mild temperatures ideal for visiting outdoor sites like the Terracotta Army. This itinerary blends Terracotta Army, Xi'an City Wall bike ride, Muslim Quarter with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
9 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Terracotta Army, Xi'an City Wall bike ride, Muslim Quarter
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Xi'an
itinerary.description: Land at Xi'an Xianyang International Airport (XIY) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Terracotta Army — Thousands of life-sized clay soldiers guarding Emperor Qin's tomb, discovered in 1974. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Xi'an: Xi'an City Wall bike ride & Big Wild Goose Pagoda
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Xi'an City Wall bike ride — Cycle the complete loop atop the best-preserved ancient city wall in China. In the afternoon, continue to Big Wild Goose Pagoda — Tang-dynasty Buddhist pagoda with an evening fountain and light show nearby. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Xi'an: Huaqing Palace & Muslim Quarter
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Huaqing Palace — Imperial hot-spring retreat at the foot of Mount Li, near the Terracotta Army. In the afternoon, continue to Muslim Quarter — Historic Hui-Muslim neighborhood of food stalls and the Great Mosque. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Mount Huashan
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Mount Huashan, a popular side trip from Xi'an. Return to Xi'an in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Shaanxi History Museum — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Xi'an: Great Mosque of Xi'an & Bell Tower and Drum Tower
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Great Mosque of Xi'an — Centuries-old mosque blending Chinese and Islamic architecture. In the afternoon, continue to Bell Tower and Drum Tower — Ming-dynasty towers at the city center, illuminated at night (exterior free, interior paid). Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Hukou Waterfall (further)
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Hukou Waterfall (further), a popular side trip from Xi'an. Return to Xi'an in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Xi'an: Terracotta Army & Xi'an City Wall bike ride
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Terracotta Army — Thousands of life-sized clay soldiers guarding Emperor Qin's tomb, discovered in 1974. In the afternoon, continue to Xi'an City Wall bike ride — Cycle the complete loop atop the best-preserved ancient city wall in China. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Hanyangling Mausoleum (lesser-visited imperial tomb) — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Big Wild Goose Pagoda or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to XIY for your departure flight.

travelTips: Visit the Terracotta Army early morning to beat both crowds and tour buses.
Rent a bike to ride the complete 13.7km loop of the City Wall — a highlight for most visitors.
Eat your way through the Muslim Quarter for the best and most affordable local food.
Combine the Terracotta Army with nearby Huaqing Palace for a fuller half-day trip.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
A VPN set up before arrival
Comfortable shoes for extensive walking and the City Wall bike ride
thingsToDo: Full loop of the City Wall by bike (13.7km)
Mount Huashan day trip (dramatic cliff-edge hiking, further afield)
Hot air balloon over the Terracotta Army site (seasonal)
Big Wild Goose Pagoda fountain show
thingsToAvoid: Visiting the Terracotta Army on a national holiday (especially Golden Week) — expect severe crowding.
Skipping a guide at the Terracotta Army — the historical context significantly enhances the visit.
Underestimating distances — the Terracotta Army is well outside the city center.
topRestaurants: Muslim Quarter food stalls — Xi'an street food (roujiamo, biangbiang noodles) ($)
De Fa Chang — Dumpling banquet restaurant ($$)
nightlife: Big Wild Goose Pagoda fountain show
instagramSpots: Muslim Quarter food stalls at night
Big Wild Goose Pagoda fountain show
hiddenGems: Huajue Alley off the main Muslim Quarter strip
familyActivities: Big Wild Goose Pagoda fountain show
adventureActivities: Full loop of the City Wall by bike (13.7km)
Mount Huashan day trip (dramatic cliff-edge hiking, further afield)
Hot air balloon over the Terracotta Army site (seasonal)
luxuryExperiences: Private guided Terracotta Army and Huaqing Palace tour
Suite stay within the ancient City Wall district
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset bike ride along the City Wall
Evening fountain show at Big Wild Goose Pagoda
budgetExperiences: Free wander through the Muslim Quarter
Bell Tower and Drum Tower exterior views
Big Wild Goose Pagoda fountain show (free to watch)
localEtiquette: A VPN arranged before arrival is essential for accessing blocked apps.
As one of China's ancient capitals, Xi'an locals take pride in the city's history — showing genuine interest is appreciated.
Most payments are cashless via WeChat Pay or Alipay; carry cash as backup.
localTransportGuide: The Xi'an Metro covers major sites including the train station and city center; the Terracotta Army is a 1-1.5 hour trip by tour bus or metro-plus-bus from downtown. Renting a bike atop the City Wall is a beloved way to see the old city's layout from above.
bestTimeToVisit: March-May and September-November, for mild temperatures ideal for visiting outdoor sites like the Terracotta Army.
weather: Semi-humid continental climate — hot summers (highs around 32-33°C) and cold, dry winters (highs around 6-7°C).
visaInformation: Most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India need a Chinese visa arranged in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~10h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Xi'an?
faqs.answer: Most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India need a Chinese visa arranged in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Xi'an?
faqs.answer: March-May and September-November, for mild temperatures ideal for visiting outdoor sites like the Terracotta Army. Semi-humid continental climate — hot summers (highs around 32-33°C) and cold, dry winters (highs around 6-7°C).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Xi'an safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Xi'an is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Xi'an?
faqs.answer: The local currency is CNY. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Sofia Cohen
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Muslim Quarter in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Emma Cohen
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Our trip to Xi'an was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Muslim Quarter was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Chen Rossi
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Terracotta Army in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Lucas Dubois
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Huaqing Palace and Muslim Quarter. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.
